rag_rules: Repair only validator diagnostics related to assignment statements and unresolved references in the current component implementation
Do not change unrelated architecture or add new components, connections, or properties
Ensure all assignment statements are valid in the component implementation scope
Verify that all referenced identifiers (ACC_INPUT_PC1, accelero_thread.acc_in_data_x, etc.) are properly declared and visible in the current scope
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex
Ensure all AGREE scalar types are valid (int, real, bool)
Align helper declaration types with the expression types they represent
Place assignment statements only in valid component implementation scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings
Use proper AGREE scalar types in declarations
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not add new components, connections, or properties to satisfy validator diagnostics
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes
AGREE constants and equation declarations in component type annexes
